# Executive Summary (auto-generated, condensed)

> NOTE: the finalize agent compressed the full plan to ~17k chars. Treat `final-plan-v2.md` / `plan7.md` as authoritative; this is a short summary only.

---

revoke coalescing verified; per-subject GDPR/FERPA erasure verified; **shared-tier `revocation_epoch`/log-tail replication-lag bound measured & published (§14.5)**.

### Phase 1.2 — Lease/log-tail replication + SpiceDB replication (only if N>2 / SLO demands)
**Build:** lease/log-tail replication fabric + cell-local SpiceDB replication + revision-translation (per Q5 verdict, treated as narrow-only cache §4.3a) + min-skew enforcement. (D5 keeps authority local; this phase only hardens replication/availability — the durable tombstone log remains the deny authority regardless of replica state.)
**Milestones:** composed authorization read p99<20ms; durable-commit p99<50ms; fail-closed-under-partition deny-minutes published (release gate); cross-region drill-down latency table verified against 6.5s SLO; SpiceDB replica confirmed narrow-only (stale-MISSING → deny, never grant).

### Phase 2 — Scale federation + sharded central index + CDC + SOC2 II + FIRST CROSS-BUYER EXPANSION
**Build:** sharded hybrid topical-macro central index (public/shared only, **privacy-bound proven in P1**, **behind the central-index PEP, encrypted at rest under control-plane keys §4.7/§11.2b**, presence DP-noised, per-consortium scope-keys, `discoverability_scope` + owner-committed `scope_epoch` enforced, opt-out default for export tenants) + materialized public-tier graph (bounded-candidate team assembly, no global PPR) + Debezium/Kafka CDC (per-record `projection_version` key, idempotent-monotonic applier, query-time **bounded-stale** `revocation_epoch`/log-tail post-filter, **lease-liveness via TTL index**, **delta log-tail replication §4.3a**) + per-shard admission control + noisy-neighbor quotas + re-clustering/re-embedding tooling + brokered-path circuit-breakers + **gray-failure injection test** + zero-copy confidential workspaces + HippoRAG2 PPR (post-AGE-verdict) + GNN team-assembly (data-gated) + Qdrant/OpenSearch shard split + **hot-tenant cell-split protocol (incl. durable-log split w/ `revocation_epoch` preservation §13.3)** + PSI (post Phase-1 usability spike) + k-anon-bucketed-overlap fallback + GDPR joint-controller arrangements + key-rotation-on-offboarding + SOC2 Type II + **export-conformant cell line + self-host edition engineering line (separately funded)** + governance machinery (N≥5) + **FIRST CROSS-BUYER EXPANSION via accession-rider template against the §2.6 procurement-veto-graph.**
**Milestones:** N≥5; **cross-buyer diversity ≥2 distinct consortia (defensible-N dimension (ii), §17)**; discovery p95<800ms under ingest; gray-failure circuit-breaker verified; central-index privacy re-validated post-scale; **central-index PEP discoverability-scope + scope_epoch + bulk-query anomaly controls live (§4.7/§10.3e)**; SOC2 Type II; PSI useful within DP ledger (or fallback shipped); cell-split exercised on synthetic; AGE/PPR benchmark verdict acted on; **defensible-N (both dimensions) reached — OR month-18 down-scope trigger fired (§17)**; **runway month-28 checkpoint: credible line-(b) ramp to ~10 confidential logos OR financing action (§16.3/R20b).**

### Phase 3 — PLG broad-acquisition surface + full grant monetization
**Build:** **PLG self-serve $179/mo** (public + own-materials, **loss-leader broad-acquisition surface — NOT a funnel for the already-closed anchor/expansion deals, §2.5b — D7**, on the §7.7-isolated pooled plane) + `mod-funding` full (grant intelligence + bounded-candidate team assembly + budget/compliance) + capped tier-pinned multi-agent (PEP-on-every-action) + per-tenant learned fusion + ISO 27001 (EU) + multi-axis pricing.
**Milestones:** ≥2 consortia under multi-year contracts (or framework LOIs signed in Phase 0–1 per §17); first cross-institution grant team assembled + funded.

---

## 19. Open Questions & Decisions to Validate

*Locked by D1–D7: grant-intelligence wedge; anchor on existing federally-funded center + DUA; single PEP chokepoint over the confidential-artifact store; owner-node-local fail-closed authority (durable-log deny, no global consensus); confidential never in shared index + pre-write red-team gate; quarantine-on-abstention default-deny; ACV ≥2–3× COGS with $179/mo PLG loss-leader; pooled non-confidential + dedicated confidential.*

1. **Gate A — anchor-grant-wedge demand (Week 1, HARD):** anchor RD office writes **≥$120k/yr minimum-viable ACV** vs a grant-program budget line, **validated against an external comparable (Cayuse/Kuali module ACV or NIH-center software line item), NOT against COGS** (§16.2); funds a paid sandbox. *Fail → pivot.*
2. **Gate B — federation/cold-start basis (Week 1, HARD):** D3 three conditions (existing DUA + N≥2 sites + recurring funded need) confirmed in writing. *Fail → Phase 1 is an independent kill-gate.*
3. **Anchor-center selection (before Phase 1, HARD):** named center meeting all four criteria (§3.4).
4. **Owner-node composed-read latency + partition deny-minutes + durable-commit latency (Phase-1 spike, RELEASE GATE):** measure the composed authorization read p99 (durable-tombstone HWM + lease + SpiceDB narrow-check), durable revocation-commit p99, and publish measured confidential deny-minutes/year — measured, not target (§4.3a/§4.4a).
5. **SpiceDB replication go/no-go (Week 1):** can ZedToken→local-revision guarantee monotonic reads under measured lag? If no → narrow-only-cache posture (durable log remains deny authority) (§7.5/§4.3a).
6. **AGE multi-hop/PPR at R1 scale (synthetic):** benchmark at N=200; exercise Neo4j/Memgraph switch via IGraphStore conformance (§9.2/§14.2).
7. **Index skew/drift + log-tail replication size (synthetic OpenAlex):** topical concentration of corpus AND queries; bounded shard set; re-cluster/re-embed cost; confirm the delta log-tail push (not bitmap) holds ≤2s p99 at N=200 worst-case bulk-revoke (§4.3a/§14.2).
8. **Central-index privacy red-team (Phase-1 pre-write DESIGN GATE, synthetic):** membership-inference + embedding-inversion + cross-tenant linkage; ε/k/churn bound in `shared/contracts/`; rollback procedure (§11.2/§11.2b).
9. **Central graph cost + team-assembly quality at N=10/50/200:** validate sharding triggers + bounded-candidate assembly.
10. **CILogon cost & eduGAIN/SAML brokering effort:** hard quote; engineer-weeks for SAML (gates campus/consortium buyers).
11. **PSI usability + scaling (Phase-1 spike):** pairwise O(N²) vs multiparty; bounded sets; confirm k-anon-bucketed fallback returns useful answers.
12. **Export-control operational surface (officer):** US-person ops + US-region + TEE conformance accepted; opt-in-per-project FRE preserved; export tenants accept `discoverability_scope=none` (§4.7).
13. **GDPR controllership + transfers + lawful basis (DPO):** EU-region pinning; opt-out-default vs opt-in/legitimate-interest for EU subjects; per-tier/per-reason max shared-tier-staleness for withdrawn consent.
14. **FERPA gating + flow-down (registrar):** owner-side + role re-check + sticky flag + audit authz sufficient; per-tier/per-reason allow-window acceptability.
15. **BYO endpoint verification + sovereign-tier definition:** in-boundary proof + mTLS + TEE-attestation matrix per pre-certified cloud; min-common `IModelProvider` version negotiation (§5.6c).
16. **PMC/Europe PMC OA-subset:** commercial-OK filter yields a usefully large corpus; ingestion/embedding cost line.
17. **Procurement (HARD Week-1 sub-condition):** named anchor CISO/contracts officer confirms **in writing** a paid sandbox pilot can sign **without full vendor security review / BAA.** If not → re-baseline runway against full-review cycle.
18. **Runway/conversion (validate):** sandbox→recurring % and months for **line (a)**; founder-led 6–9 month first-close; **line (b) confidential-federation close dated at month 12–18 with legal-review path costed (§2.6/§16.3)**; consortium-BD before Phase 1.
19. **TEE deployment surface:** Nitro vs SEV-SNP/TDX abstraction + attestation; spike when first export-class buyer's threat model demands.
20. **Eval gold-set bootstrapping per tenant:** built inside the cell, who labels; in-boundary PEP-gated judge cost; confirm traces are KEK-encrypted + shred on crypto-shred (§9.3/§11.3b).
21. **PLG locked-requirement sign-off:** confirm "individual PIs as PLG top-of-funnel" at $179/mo is a loss-leader broad-acquisition surface (Phase 3, NOT a funnel for the anchor/expansion motion, §2.5b), not standalone revenue — surface now if a stakeholder disagrees.
22. **Race-window vs cross-buyer-N (Findings 8,9):** decide by **month 18** whether the ramp shows a credible path to defensible-N (N≥5 AND ≥2 distinct consortia) by month 24 — if not, fire the regulated-niche down-scope (§17). Sign a **second-consortium framework LOI** early to de-risk dimension (ii).
23. **Vault/KMS isolation + confidential-derivative/draft/eval encryption (Findings 3-eval, 4):** confirm cloud-KMS per-tenant + tenant-CMK volume encryption satisfy the anchor's key-isolation requirement AND that KEK crypto-shred renders vector/BM25/graph derivatives + generated drafts + eval traces unreadable; self-run Vault sovereign/on-prem only (no Community namespaces) (§11.3/§11.3b).
24. **Confidential-tier COGS + GPU density validation (Findings 10,11):** confirm **MIG K=2 hardware partitioning** holds the in-boundary + no-shared-KV-cache isolation requirement to a CISO AND keeps COGS at **$54k/yr**; confirm the **small (8–13B quantized) confidential model passes the RAGAS faithfulness gate on a MIG slice** — if not, fall back to dedicated-GPU **$78k/yr** + Sovereign pricing for all confidential tenants; confirm Sovereign priced ≥$160k (≥2× $78k) / ≥$175k (55% margin); validate the K-amortization with the anchor's actual confidential-corpus size and query load (§16.1/§16.2).
25. **Pooled-plane PgBouncer mode (Finding 5):** confirm the production PgBouncer mode is compatible with `SET LOCAL` transaction-scoped tenant context with no cross-borrow leakage; validate the object-authz Check latency budget on the pooled plane (§7.7/§13.1).
26. **Expansion procurement durations (Finding 8):** validate the §2.6 per-stakeholder time-to-yes (OGC/IRB/export/CISO) with the anchor's own counsel and the FIRST candidate expansion institution; confirm the accession-rider template is acceptable to a second institution's OGC.
27. **Line-(a) ACV + runway-to-line-(b) financing (Finding 8/line-a, R20b):** validate the **$20–60k realistic line-(a) institutional ACV** and win-rate against Atom/Pivot/Instrumentl/free-VIVO in the anchor network; confirm seed funds the path to **~10 line-(b) confidential logos** (NOT made breakeven by line (a)); decide the financing trigger (≥$4–5M seed or faster confidential close) if line (b) slips past month 18 (§16.3).
28. **Broker topology sign-off (Finding 6/broker):** confirm the data-access broker's DB role is scoped to the confidential-artifact/classification/grant tables ONLY and cannot read any feature module's operational schema; confirm the broker-role contract test (§4.2/§15.2) holds as modules are added in Phase 1.0+.
29. **Kernel cross-node interface evolution (Finding 6/kernel):** confirm the min-common-version negotiation for `IModelProvider`/`IIndexProfile`/`IExchangeFeed` is exercised once (a synthetic additive field) before N≥5, and that the kernel-package compat CI check catches a non-compatible change (§5.6c).

---

*End of plan v7 (re-anchored + adversarial-review-hardened). Locked decisions D1–D7 are unchanged. The eleven in-scope findings are resolved in the body, each within the relevant locked decision:*

1. *(Finding 1 — lease-vs-log composition / zero-window contradiction) §4.3a pins ONE decision order: the durable tombstone high-water-mark is the SOLE authority for the DENY dimension, read synchronously cell-local on every confidential read → genuine zero allow-window for all revocation reasons; the lease and the SpiceDB-local-replica are demoted to narrow-only positive caches that can deny-earlier but never allow-wider. §4.3's latency budget collapses the three into one 20ms cell-local composed read (no consensus hop, D5 intact); §14.5's allow-window row is corrected to ZERO for confidential, the 2s bound applying only to shared-tier discovery; the §7.3 "ABAC narrows-only" invariant is generalized to "caches narrow-only."*
2. *(Finding 2 — epoch overloaded across two granularities) §4.3a/§10.3a disambiguate into a per-CELL fenced `revocation_epoch` (versions the tombstone set / log-tail snapshot; detects backward recovery) and a per-RECORD `projection_version` (the CDC applier's lower-version-reject value), eliminating the over-/under-reject ambiguity; the replicated object is a delta tombstone-LOG-TAIL (proportional to revocation rate, ≪1KB steady-state, ~160KB worst-case bulk-revoke), NOT a ~10–12MB per-record bitmap, re-justifying the ≤2s p99 push at N=200.*
3. *(Finding 3 — eval path un-PEP-gated confidential side-door) §9.3/§11.3b make eval gold-sets, stored RAGAS traces, and judge I/O first-class confidential-tier artifacts inside the cell, tenant-KEK-encrypted (so they crypto-shred and are covered by per-subject erasure), routed through the single PEP, with the judge-model bound to the §8.1/§8.2 routing table (confidential eval cannot select a cloud judge) and a contract test enforcing it.*
4. *(Finding 4 — generated draft not classified/KEK-bound) §6.1/§8.5/§11.3b make the generated draft, autosave, version history, synthesizer intermediate buffers, and the RAG cache of confidential context first-class confidential artifacts whose tier is derived by the MAX-rule over grounding sources and which persist only in tenant-KEK-encrypted stores, added to the §11.3b enumeration and the post-crypto-shred zero-decryptable-hits contract test.*
5. *(Finding 5 — share-correctness asymmetry) §4.7/§10.3e add the additive mirror of revoke-correctness: `discoverability_scope` carries an owner-committed monotonic `scope_epoch`; a projection is discoverable at a scope only after the owner durably commits it; the central-index PEP serves at the narrower owner-committed scope (caches narrow-only, never stale-HIGH), with a property test that no projection is ever queryable wider than the owner's currently-committed scope across version-skew/replay. (Pooled-plane per-tenant isolation, the original Finding-5 item, remains resolved at §7.7.)*
6. *(Finding 6 — broker-topology contradiction, projection field-schema, and kernel-interface evolution) §4.2 pins the broker as a confidentiality/egress-projection plane scoped to the shared confidential-artifact/classification/grant tables ONLY — NOT a holder of every module's credentials; feature-module operational schemas stay behind their own DB roles; "single PEP" = one logical PDP with per-location enforcement adapters; a broker-role contract test enforces it and the fan-in is fixed regardless of module count. §5.6b versions the PublishableProjection field-schema (registry-backed, backward+forward compat, recognize-before-emit, consumer registry + exercised removal). §5.6c extends evolution discipline to ALL kernel interfaces, classifying intra-cell-only vs cross-node and giving `IModelProvider`/`IIndexProfile`/`IExchangeFeed` min-common-version negotiation + a kernel-package compat CI check.*
7. *(Findings 7, 10, 11 — D7 unit-economics, COGS-table reconciliation, GPU density) §16.1 recomputes Table B totals from its own line items (shared-GPU-with-MIG = $54k/yr; Sovereign = $78k/yr), withdrawing the contradictory $36–43k/$66–72k totals; §16.2 re-derives the D7 ratio honestly ($120k / $54k = 2.22×, D7-COMPLIANT for the shared edition; dedicated-GPU at $120k = 1.54×, D7-VIOLATING → Sovereign priced ≥$160k), states the 60% gate is missed at $120k (true margin 55%) and resolves it (gate set to 55% at the $120k floor, target $135k+ for 60%), and separates the 2–3×-COGS gross-margin floor from the CAC+GTM+compliance-loaded $120k minimum-viable ACV validated against an external comparable. The K=2-vs-isolation tension is resolved by MIG hardware partitioning (per-tenant GPU slice + own vLLM process, no shared KV-cache side channel) with the small-model RAGAS-faithfulness fallback to the $78k dedicated-GPU basis (Q24).*
8. *(Finding 8 — line-(a) runway over-claim, expansion friction, PLG sequencing) §16.3 quantifies line-(a) ACV at a realistic $20–60k (commodity public-assistant, not $120k), stress-tests the runway against it, and re-cuts the model so seed funds the path to line-(b) breakeven (~10 confidential logos) with line (a) as a partial offset only — flagging a larger-seed/faster-close financing trigger (R20b/Q27) if line (b) slips past month 18; §2.6 keeps the expansion procurement-veto-graph + per-institution time-to-yes; §2.5b resolves the PLG inconsistency by making the anchor/expansion motion founder-led direct BD and repositioning PLG as a Phase-3 broad-acquisition surface (NOT a funnel for deals that close before it ships), removing the unsupported champion-seeding claim for Phase 0–2.*
9. *(Finding 9 — defensibility fallback concreteness) §1/§17 make defensible-N two-dimensional (N≥5 AND ≥2 distinct consortia), name the distribution-acquisition plan, and replace any silent network-effect fallback at sub-threshold N with a dated month-18 regulated-niche down-scope trigger.*
